parameters.jsCode:
const { jobs, count } = $input.first().json;

if (!count || count === 0) {
  return [{ json: { jobs: [], count: 0, prompt: null } }];
}

const jobsList = jobs.map((j, i) => {
  const sal = j.salary_min
    ? '$' + j.salary_min.toLocaleString() + '-$' + (j.salary_max ? j.salary_max.toLocaleString() : '?')
    : (j.salary_text || 'Not listed');
  return `${i+1}. Title: "${j.title}" | Company: "${j.company}" | Salary: ${sal} | Source: ${j.source}\nDescription: ${j.description.substring(0, 500)}`;
}).join('\n\n');

const prompt = `You are a job match scorer for a specific candidate. Analyze each job and return structured JSON.

CANDIDATE PROFILE — Gadget
- Location: Remote-only (WA state), 10% max travel — hard requirement
- Experience: ~20 years in networking, security, and infrastructure — NOT a software developer
- Current Role: Data Centers Network Engineer, Expeditors International (2025–present)
  • Enterprise network design/implementation across 350 locations, 19,000+ employees
  • Cisco WLC and Juniper MIST wireless; Cisco Nexus and HP/Aruba switching
  • Managing Express Routes to cloud providers (not provisioning)
  • Leading Fortinet SD-WAN deployment across entire Expeditors network (in progress)

- STRONG / EXPERT skills:
  • Routing: BGP, OSPF, MPLS, DMVPN, VRRP, STP
  • Switching: VLANs, QoS, ACLs, Cisco Nexus, HP/Aruba
  • Wireless: Cisco Meraki (enterprise deployment + 6-month ownership + 3yr homelab), Cisco WLC, Juniper MIST
  • VPN: Site-to-site, remote access, WireGuard, DMVPN
  • WAN/SD-WAN: MPLS circuits, Express Routes (management), Fortinet SD-WAN (active deployment)
  • Firewalls: Cisco ASA, Palo Alto, Juniper, Checkpoint — config, rule lifecycle, hardening
  • Voice/UC: Avaya VoIP — 6 years at State of NM (call routing, dial plans, SIP trunking); Cisco UCM
  • IPS: Tipping Point IPS
  • Compliance: PCI-DSS — network segmentation, cardholder environment controls
  • Auth: Juniper RADIUS, Linux iptables
  • Monitoring: Prometheus, Grafana, Loki, SolarWinds Orion, Splunk (analyst/end-user, not admin)
  • Systems: Linux (RHEL/CentOS), Windows Server; VMware, Proxmox; Docker, Kubernetes
  • Automation/Proxy: Traefik v3, CrowdSec, n8n; Ansible (in training), Python (in training)
  • Vendors: Cisco, Juniper, Palo Alto, Checkpoint, Cisco ASA, Fortinet

- LIMITED / DEVELOPING:
  • F5 LTM/GTM: conceptual only — understands concepts, has NOT personally administered
  • Cloud provisioning: Azure Express Routes managed (not built); AWS/GCP awareness only
  • Splunk: end-user/analyst only, not platform admin
  • Ansible + Python: actively learning — NOT a programmer, do NOT score as developer

- Certifications: CCNA (97%), BICSI, B.A.S. Information Security, B.A.S. Electronics/Communications Engineering
- Salary: $140k minimum (hard floor — no exceptions)
- Hard dealbreakers: Not remote, requires clearance, requires drug testing, travel >10%

TARGET ROLES (good fit):
SRE (infrastructure/reliability focused), Senior Network Engineer, Cloud Network Engineer,
Infrastructure Engineer, Platform Engineer, Network-focused DevOps, Network Security Engineer,
Systems Administrator (senior), Network Architect

NOT A FIT FOR:
Software engineering, web development, application development, data engineering, ML/AI engineering,
any role where writing production code is the PRIMARY responsibility

JOBS TO EVALUATE:
${jobsList}

SCORING RULES — apply these strictly before assigning a score:

AUTOMATIC DISQUALIFIERS (score 20 or below):
- Role is primarily software/application/web development
- Requires 3+ years of programming as primary skill (Python/Go/Java as main job function, not scripting)
- Frontend/React/Angular/mobile required
- Job title or description is clearly for a software engineer, not infra/ops
- Requires clearance or drug testing
- Not remote / requires relocation

PENALTIES:
- Minus 25: heavy coding requirements (Go/Rust/Java required, or "you will build software")
- Minus 20: requires AWS Solutions Architect or developer certifications (not network certs)
- Minus 15: requires CS degree or software engineering background specifically
- Minus 10: scripting/automation required (Python OK as secondary, but note as gap)
- Minus 15: salary clearly below $140k

BONUSES:
- Plus 10: network-centric SRE (focuses on network reliability, BGP/routing observability, firewall automation)
- Plus 8: mentions Cisco/Juniper/Palo Alto/Fortinet or specific network protocol experience
- Plus 8: Avaya/VoIP/UC experience valued
- Plus 5: infrastructure/platform role that is clearly ops-heavy rather than dev-heavy
- Plus 5: Grafana/Prometheus/observability stack prominent in requirements
- Plus 5: PCI-DSS or compliance-focused network security

Return ONLY a valid JSON array — no markdown fences, no extra text:
[{
  "index": 1,
  "score": 85,
  "fit_summary": "Exactly two sentences explaining the match quality and key alignment.",
  "missing_skills": "comma-separated list of required skills not in profile, or None",
  "red_flags": "salary below $140k, travel requirement, clearance, heavy coding required, etc — or None",
  "tags": ["SRE", "Kubernetes", "AWS", "Grafana"],
  "role_type": "SRE",
  "resume_tips": [
    "Emphasize your BGP/MPLS experience in the summary",
    "Lead with Docker/Kubernetes hands-on work",
    "Add Ansible learning progress to skills section"
  ],
  "cover_letter_hook": "Two to three sentences for an opening paragraph tailored specifically to this company and role."
}]

Scoring guide: 90-100=perfect fit, 75-89=strong match, 60-74=good candidate, 45-59=possible stretch, <45=poor fit (do NOT send to Notion).
role_type must be one of: SRE, Network Engineer, DevOps, Platform Engineer, Cloud Engineer, Infrastructure Engineer, Other
tags: up to 8 key technology/skill tags extracted directly from the job description
resume_tips: 3-5 specific actionable bullets referencing Gadget's actual network/infra experience — include relevant confirmed skills not yet on resume (Meraki, Avaya VoIP, OSPF/MPLS, PCI-DSS, Palo Alto, Fortinet) where they fit
cover_letter_hook: reference the company or role specifically, not generic`;

return [{ json: { jobs, prompt } }];

parameters.body: ={{ JSON.stringify({ contents: [ { parts: [ { text: ($json.prompt || 'Score 0 jobs: []') } ] } ], generationConfig: { temperature: 0.2, responseMimeType: 'application/json' } }) }}

parameters.name: title_filter
parameters.value: "network engineer" OR "site reliability engineer" OR "infrastructure engineer" OR "network security engineer" OR "platform engineer" OR "devops engineer" OR "systems engineer" OR "cloud engineer" OR "sre" OR "hybrid cloud engineer"

parameters.name: what_or
parameters.value: devops sre infrastructure platform reliability network hybrid

parameters.name: search
parameters.value: network OR sre OR reliability OR infrastructure OR sysadmin OR noc

parameters.name: query
parameters.value: site reliability engineer OR network reliability engineer infrastructure remote

parameters.name: query
parameters.value: senior network engineer OR network security engineer OR cloud network engineer OR infrastructure security engineer OR hybrid cloud engineer remote

parameters.name: query
parameters.value: site reliability engineer OR senior network engineer OR network infrastructure engineer OR cloud network engineer OR hybrid cloud engineer
